title: ATOMIC: delete 30 regex sanitizer Vecs + 24 obsolete tests + flip dispatch + M3-FIND-01 string-literal filter

phase_b_string_literal_filter.approach: M3-FIND-01 mitigation. The raw-substring fallback in `member_patterns_match` (`pat_rcv.is_empty() && descendant_text.contains(pat_field)`) was matching against descendant text that included string-literal child text. We now compute `masked_text` per descendant (string-literal byte ranges replaced with ASCII spaces, preserving offsets) and pass that to `member_patterns_match`. The structural paths in that helper re-extract from real AST nodes via `extract_member_access_receiver_and_field` and `extract_call_name`, so masking only affects the raw-substring fallback path. `member_patterns_match` itself was NOT modified.

phase_c_empty_regex_sanitizer_vecs.notes: Each `sanitizers: vec![...]` body replaced with `vec![]`. Comment in each bank documents the AST equivalent (e.g., `int|float|bool` → PYTHON_AST_SANITIZERS, `parseInt|Number|parseFloat` → TYPESCRIPT_AST_SANITIZERS, etc.). LUA_PATTERNS is shared by Lua + Luau, RUBY_PATTERNS retains its `\bgets\b` UserInput source regex (out-of-scope for sanitizer milestone).

phase_d_dispatch_flip.comments_updated: M2 'AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK' comments at process_block, ssa_propagate, compute_taint_with_tree, build_sanitizer_ast_index, and SsaPropagateCtx all rewritten to 'AST-only (regex bank deleted; M2 fallback removed)'.

unused_params_removed.rationale: Post-flip these parameters had zero uses; clippy `-D warnings` flagged them. Per the no-gaming rule, removed entirely instead of `_`-prefixing (the values are genuinely no longer needed).

sanitize_breaks_flow_per_language.transitions: 14 string-literal regression-guards transitioned RED→GREEN (M3-FIND-01 mitigation succeeds); 19 regular sanitizer-truncates-flow tests still GREEN; 4 closes-#24-shaped baseline regression tests still GREEN.

sanitize_breaks_flow_ast_only_harness.note: All 20 ast_only mirror tests still GREEN — AST-only path unchanged in behaviour for the regular sanitize-flow scenarios.

security_tests.note: All sanitizer-related tests pass; deleted 4 obsolete; 33 passing tests cover preserved behaviour including test_compute_taint_sanitizer_removes_taint and test_sanitizer_type_serialization.

taint_tests_inline.note: All taint_tests in src/security/taint_tests.rs pass (excluding the 20 obsolete ones we deleted). test_sanitizer_removes_taint, test_no_vulnerability_when_sanitized, test_propagate_through_*, etc. all GREEN.

pre_existing_unrelated_failures.reason: Documented pre-M5 RED in the test file's own header (lines 6-27): rr_module_function_integ_test fixtures FAIL at HEAD pre-M2/M3/M4 because the structured-shape rewrites in RUBY_AST_*/ELIXIR_AST_*/OCAML_AST_* haven't been done yet. Out of scope for sanitizer-removal-v1. Bare `gets` parses as identifier (not call), so AST `call_names: ['gets']` doesn't fire under AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE.
pre_existing_unrelated_failures.evidence: M4 only modifies sanitizer dispatch; Ruby source detection (RUBY_AST_SOURCES) was not touched. The `\bgets\b` regex in RUBY_PATTERNS sources is preserved.

pre_existing_unrelated_failures.tests: tree_handles_empty_directory, test_realistic_python_project_structure, test_git_log_*, p2_language_status_tests::*, test_cognitive_else_not_counted, test_compute_martin_metrics_empty, test_analyze_dead_code_empty, test_find_similar_no_clones, test_parse_coverage_*
pre_existing_unrelated_failures.reason: Pre-existing failures in unrelated crates (tree, fs, git, surface, metrics, quality). M4 changes are confined to crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs + taint_tests.rs + security_tests.rs.
Pre-existing doctest issues in unrelated modules (Kotlin/Luau code blocks parsed as Rust, missing tldr_core::contracts module).

cargo_clippy_deny_warnings.note: Initial run flagged 4 unused-variable warnings post-dispatch-flip (stmt, line_stmt, language). Per no-gaming rule, parameters were removed (genuinely unused), not _-prefixed.

binary_verify.expected: real int(raw) sanitizer correctly clears taint between UserInput source and CodeEval sink
binary_verify.note: Confirms AST-only sanitizer dispatch works end-to-end.

m3_find_01_mitigation.approach: Implemented as text-masking inside `build_sanitizer_ast_index`, NOT as ancestor-walking from the matched node. The matching descendants are typically the assignment node itself (which is NOT inside a string literal — `is_in_string` only checks ancestors). The text-masking approach replaces string-literal byte ranges with ASCII spaces in a copy of the descendant's text before passing to `member_patterns_match`. This neutralizes the raw-substring fallback's `pat_rcv.is_empty() && descendant_text.contains(pat_field)` path while leaving the structural paths (call-shape, member-access) untouched (they re-extract from real AST nodes).
m3_find_01_mitigation.tradeoff: Allocates a new String per descendant. Acceptable because `build_sanitizer_ast_index` is called once per `compute_taint_with_tree` invocation, and descendant count is bounded by AST size — no quadratic blowup.
m3_find_01_mitigation.verification: All 14 string-literal regression-guard tests transitioned RED→GREEN. 4 binary smoke tests across Python/TS/Ruby/Rust confirm zero false positives on string-literal-only fixtures. Positive control (Python `int(raw)` real sanitizer) confirms taint flow still correctly broken.

preserved_public_api.detect_sanitizer: Iterates empty Vec → always returns None. Public for external callers.
preserved_public_api.find_sanitizers_in_statement: Iterates empty Vec → always returns empty Vec.
preserved_public_api.rationale: These functions are #[pub] and may be invoked by external crates; preserving them as no-ops satisfies the rule about API stability while allowing the regex banks to be empty. Internal dispatch (process_block, ssa_propagate) no longer calls them — only the AST sanitizer index is consulted.
